metadata.title: Philadelphia City All-Star Football Game Database
metadata.description: Comprehensive historical records of Public vs. Non-Public All-Star games (1975-2019)
metadata.source: Compiled from OCR, text scraping, and historical records

scoring_summary: Public-Safery (Nedley and Tuck tackled Daley in end zone)
games.notes: Parochial-Morrin 41 pass from Maley (Sheridan extra point) - Parochial-Citrone 8 run (Kick failed) - Public-Murphy 5 run (run failed)
highlights: Before overflow crowd of 14,426 at Northeast High
Jack Maley 41-yard TD pass to Mike Morrin (first TD in All-Star history)
John Citrone 8-yard TD run
Brett Egan (West Catholic) 13 tackles
Tim Murphy (Lincoln) 5-yard keeper TD for Public
games.recap: Before an overflow crowd of 14,426 at Northeast High, Jack Maley (Penn Charter) passed 3-for-6 for 79 yards and produced the first TD in All-Star Game history with a 41-yard pass to Mike Morrin (North Catholic).

games.notes: Pub-Benson 1 run (Freiling kick); Par-Kearns 10 pass from McKay (pass failed); Pub-Montgomery 7 run (Freiling kick)
highlights: Strong defense limited Non-Public to minus-14 total offense yards
Nate Montgomery 14 rushes for 53 yards, TD with 43 seconds left
Bob Keels (Central) 3 catches for 37 yards
Lawrence Reid (Dougherty) 9 rushes for 27 yards
Mike McKay (Judge) TD pass to Tom Kearns
games.recap: Stellar defense by James Parker (Franklin), Theodore Smith III (West Phila.) and Anthony Scott (University City) limited Non-Public to minus-14 yards total offense.

scoring_summary: P - McClelland 15 run (White pass from Hodges)
NP - Lafferty 17 pass from Paulone (Smith pass from Paulone)
games.officials: Tom (Goalpost) Elliott, Charles Allen, Bob Bram, Ralph McNally, Frank Monaco, Paul Baker
games.notes: P - McClelland 15 run (White pass from Hodges); NP - Lafferty 17 pass from Paulone (Smith pass from Paulone)
highlights: Non-Public tied game in 4th quarter
Mike Paulone 17-yard TD pass to Paul Lafferty
Paulone conversion pass to Jerry Smith (Neumann)
Leo McClelland 19-85 rushing, TD
Michael Gold (Bartram) 18-51 rushing
games.recap: Non-Public gained the tie in the fourth quarter when Mike Paulone, of the Pennsylvania School for the Deaf, completed a 17-yard TD pass to Paul Lafferty (Judge).

highlights: John Loughery 3-for-4, 72 yards, 2 TDs
Ed Clark (Judge) and Bob Loscalzo (Dougherty) TD passes
Tim Gramlich 2 catches for 78 yards, 2 TDs
Brian Ewell 13 rushes for 79 yards, TD
Bob Perry (Gratz) 2 fumble recoveries
games.recap: Before 28,432 fans at Franklin Field, John Loughery (Penn Charter) completed three of four passes for 72 yards and two TDs.

Joe McErlean TD

games.recap: Harry Ulmer (North Catholic) rushed 14 times for 138 yards and a TD at Franklin Field. Joe McErlean (SJ Prep) ran for the other TD.

mvps.stats: 58 yards, 1 TD on 20 carries, 100 yards on 6 returns

highlights: Ron Bryant 2-yard TD with 2:23 left (game-winner)
Todd Mitchell 20 rushes for 97 yards
Kenyatta Rush (Central) 8 tackles
Rick Hollawell (Ryan) 58 yards and TD, 100 return yards
Vince Grimes (Judge) 9 tackles
games.recap: Ron Bryant (King) caught a 2-yard TD pass from Keith Singleton (Washington) with 2:23 left to break a 6-6 tie. Todd Mitchell (King) rushed 20 times for 97 yards.

highlights: Al Settembrino 26 rushes for 114 yards, 2 TDs
Tim Cunniff 20-yard FG with 3:28 left (game-winner)
Tom Erwin (Ryan) 9 tackles
Rich Drayton (Central) 2 catches for 70 yards

mvps.name: Al Settembrino
mvps.stats: 26 rushes for 114 yards, 2 TDs, 61 return yards

games.recap: Al Settembrino (Ryan) rushed 26 times for 114 yards and two TDs, and added 61 yards on returns, and Tim Cunniff (Ryan) made the difference by kicking a 20-yard field goal with 3:28 left.

highlights: First All-Star Game played at night
Jerome 'Boxcar' Bailey scored only TD with 2:57 before halftime
Harun Reed 10 tackles, 1 interception
Roger Talley 3 interceptions for Public
Chris Fagan 10-for-25, 66 yards
Rob Gentile (West Catholic) 6 catches for 49 yards
games.recap: In the first All-Star Game to be played at night, Jerome 'Boxcar' Bailey (Bok) ran for a 1-yard TD 2:57 before halftime to account for the scoring.

highlights: Jeff Simmins 9 rushes for 56 yards, 2 TDs (vs 50 yards season high)
Frank Wycheck 11 rushes for 68 yards, TD
Frank Altmire (Ryan) 2 interceptions
Patrick Tomosky (Roxborough) 5-for-10, 129 yards
Keita Crespina 4 catches for 100 yards
games.recap: Jeff Simmins (Judge), whose highest output during the season had been 50 yards, rushed nine times for 56 yards and two TDs. Frank Wycheck (Ryan) added 68 yards and a TD on 11 carries.

highlights: Charles Way defense forced 5 turnovers
Dave MacDonald (Lincoln) 2 TD passes
Public scored first 13 points
80-yard TD pass wiped out by penalty
Frank Costa (SJ Prep) 10-for-20, 83 yards, 2 TDs
games.recap: Charles Way (Northeast) made seven tackles to key a defense that forced five turnovers. Dave MacDonald (Lincoln) passed for two TDs as Public scored the first 13 points.

highlights: Mark Ostaszewski and Derrick Norris combined for 135 yards total offense
Two N-P TDs on defense
Darrin MacMillan (Dougherty) fumble recovery in end zone TD
Mike Conway (Dougherty) 7-yard fumble return TD
Mica Dawkins 89-yard kickoff return TD for Public
games.recap: Halfback Mark Ostaszewski (Ryan) and quarterback Derrick Norris (Dougherty) combined for 135 yards total offense, though neither scored. Two N-P TDs came on defense.

highlights: Scotty Mack 9 carries for 116 yards, 2 TDs
Stacey Lane 7 tackles with 4 TFLs
Non-Public rushing attack netted minus-9 yards on 30 carries

mvps.name: Scotty Mack
mvps.stats: 9 carries for 116 yards, 2 TDs (20 and 56 yards)

games.recap: Scotty Mack (Roxborough) carried nine times for 116 yards and touchdowns of 20 and 56 yards. Stacey Lane (Olney) led the defense with seven tackles.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file (cityallstarbox93.jpg) on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
highlights: First All-Star game played in May (previous 18 in April)
Brian Kennedy 1-yard TD with 24 seconds remaining
Troy Kersey 6 catches for 100 yards
James 'Noot' Smith (Gratz) 12 rushes for 63 yards and TD
games.recap: A 1-yard run by Brian Kennedy (Judge) with 24 seconds remaining decided the first All-Star game played in May. The first 18 had been played in April.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file (cityallstarbox94.jpg) on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
highlights: Jess Sodaski rushed 9 times for 57 yards and TD
Lance Shaw rushed 20 times for 100 yards, 2 TDs
Shawn McNesby led N-P defense with 9 tackles
Jonathan 'Country' Perry had 11 tackles including 4 for losses and FF

mvps.name: Jess Sodaski
mvps.stats: 9 rushes for 57 yards, 1 TD; 4-for-7 passing (56 yards)

games.recap: Quarterback Jess Sodaski (SJ Prep) rushed nine times for 57 yards and a touchdown and passed 4-for-7 for 56 yards, while schoolmate Lance Shaw rushed 20 times for 100 yards and two TDs.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file (cityallstarbox95.jpg) on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
highlights: Spencer Whetts 1-yard sneak TD
Whetts 6-for-7 passing with TD to Javer Dillard
Hassan Brockman 38-yard reverse TD with 3:20 left (winning TD)
Dan Cliggett (Washington) added 3rd PAT
Jim Emanuel (Ryan) made 11 tackles for N-P
games.recap: Spencer Whetts (Germantown) scored the game's first TD on a 1-yard sneak and passed 6-for-7 for 71 yards and another TD to his high school teammate, Javer Dillard.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file (cityallstarbox96.jpg) on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
highlights: With 2:30 remaining N-P trailing 6-3
Tim Dumas caught high snap as holder and scampered 12 yards for TD
Scott Palanjian earlier kicked 37-yard FG
Chris Kennedy rushed 18 times for 69 yards
Kevin Campbell (Ryan) made 9 tackles
Samuel Bookard (Washington) 52-yard interception return TD, blocked FG
Marcus Godfrey (Mastbaum) 15 rushes for 67 yards
games.recap: With just over 2:30 remaining, N-P was trailing, 6-3, and Scott Palanjian (SJ Prep) was preparing to attempt a 29-yard field goal. But after catching a high snap, holder Tim Dumas (Ryan) took off.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file (cityallstarbox97.jpg) on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
highlights: George Radu set FG record with 3 makes and 5 kickoffs to at least 13-yard line
Ed Giddings 80-yard TD flanker reverse
Steve Szydlik 10-for-17 passing
Mike Crawford (Mastbaum) 7 tackles, blocked FG
Kevin Joyce (Neumann) caught TD for N-P
games.recap: George Radu (Washington) set a game record by hammering field goals of 27, 37 and 31 yards and sent all five of his kickoffs to at least the 13-yard line.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file (cityallstarbox98.jpg) on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
highlights: Mike Prince made 5 catches for 90 yards
Joe Corsanico 48-yard catch setup winning TD
Jeff Moore scored winning TD with 1:18 remaining
Mike Gavaghan (Ryan) kicked 27-yard FG
Victor Hobson (SJ Prep) 8 tackles, 1 catch, 1 return
Paul Northern (Bartram) scored first TD
games.recap: Mike Prince (Dougherty) made five receptions for 90 yards and had a 48-yard catch from Joe Corsanico (Ryan) that set up the winning touchdown, a 1-yard run by Jeff Moore (Ryan) with 1:18 remaining.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file (cityallstarbox99.jpg) on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
highlights: Brett Singleton (Dobbins), Mark Horton (Germantown), Scott Smith (Washington) stopped Stephen Piner at 6-inch line on 4th-goal
Andre Davis (Dobbins) 95-yard fumble return TD
Davis blocked punt recovery prevented N-P TD
Kairi Cooper rushed 18 times for 60 yards
Edvard Jean threw TD to David Kenner (Frankford)
Jim Jankiewicz (SJ Prep) made 5 receptions for 50 yards

mvps.name: Andre Davis
mvps.stats: 10 tackles, 57-yard kickoff return, 95-yard fumble return TD

games.recap: The game ended in dramatic fashion as linemen Brett Singleton (Dobbins), Mark Horton (Germantown) and Scott Smith (Washington) combined to stop Stephen Piner (West Catholic) at the 6-inch line on fourth-and-goal from the 2.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file (cityallstar00.jpg) on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
highlights: Ben Dever and Kevin Stefanski combined 10-for-18, 137 yards
James Berry (Penn Charter) and Fran Semon (North Catholic) ran for TDs
Public limited to 56 yards on 33 plays
Mike Walls (Ryan) 6 tackles (2 for 13 yards in losses)
Brian Kulb (North) 5 tackles, 1 interception
Paul Ragan (Judge) 37-yard FG
John Grone (Mastbaum) and Tony Felice (Northeast) scored for Public
games.recap: Ben Dever (Dougherty) and Kevin Stefanski (SJ Prep) combined to pass 10-for-18 for 137 yards, James Berry (Penn Charter) and Fran Semon (North Catholic) ran for TDs.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
highlights: Public posted just 4th shutout in series history
Teddy Lipford-Jose Pagan 80-yard TD (new record)
Roger Price 44-yard FG (scoring record 2nd mark)
Ivan Kosty and Rodney McCarter (Bok) had interceptions
Timothy King (Edison) recovered fumble
Shawn Williams (Frankford) made 9 tackles
games.recap: Two records were set as Public posted just the fourth shutout in series history. The first mark came when Teddy Lipford (Central) hit Jose Pagan (Edison) with an 80-yard scoring pass.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
highlights: N-P fell into 14-0 hole then stormed to victory
SJ Prep teammates Jim Lachman and Pete Chromiak made TD catches
Justin O'Brien (Judge) kicked 31-yard FG
Rich Ott (Ryan) and Mike Piotrowicz (North Catholic) had 8 and 7 tackles
Omar Sanders (Overbrook) and Ryan Gore (Washington) ran for Public TDs
Maurice Bennett (Washington) and Anthony Wynder (Mastbaum) had 10 tackles each
games.recap: N-P fell into a 14-0 hole, then stormed to victory behind a pair of passing TDs and a 31-yard field goal by Justin O'Brien (Judge).

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
highlights: Pat Kaiser (SJ Prep) rushed 22 times for 75 yards, 2 TDs
Kaiser's scoring came in first half (3 and 8-yard TDs)
Steve Sandberg (Northeast) kicked 22-yard FG for Public
Johnny Ortiz (Roman) 3-yard TD pass from Matt Ryan
Fumble recoveries by Mike Gillespie (Neumann) and Stephen Sizer (SJ Prep) setup Kaiser TDs
N-P forced 5 turnovers total
Darrell Turner (Frankford) 6-for-15, 112 yards for Public

mvps.name: Matt Ryan
mvps.stats: TD pass to Johnny Ortiz (3 yards)

games.recap: Pat Kaiser (SJ Prep) rushed 22 times for 75 yards and two TDs, hammered two PAT and made a 31-yard reception as N-P claimed a second consecutive win.

games.note: Boxscore exists as image file on tedsilary.com but content is not text-extractable
highlights: Curtis Brinkley set city game record 399 yards rushing (historical season: 2,813; career: 7,413)
Brinkley first player in all-star annals to score 3 TDs
Andre Sloan-El shattered passing yards record (129 previous)
Matt Parkhurst set reception record with 7 catches
Dale Curry-Carl Peterson (Judge) 80-yard TD reception (record-tying)
Rich McFillin (Washington) threw 83-yard TD to Michael Washington
Gunshots rang out in parking lot with 2:21 left (game continued)

mvps.name: Curtis Brinkley
mvps.stats: 3 rushing TDs (first in all-star history), 55 yards on 15 carries

games.recap: The N-P's high-profile stars led the way on a night when records were smashed in routine fashion. Curtis 'Boonah' Brinkley (West Catholic), the leading rusher in city history for a game (399), became the first player in all-star annals to score three TDs.

games.notes: Jerry Butler (Washington) recorded 13.3 yards per carry with 3 rushing TDs
highlights: Public impressively halted 3-game losing streak
Jerry Butler's 79-yard TD run came minute before halftime
Sammy Tranks set record with 67-yard interception return TD
Tranks' 42-yard catch began Pub's last scoring drive
Cornelius Bunch (Central) posted 2.5 sacks among 9 tackles
Cory Moultrie (Bok) made 2 of team's 5 interceptions
Steve Quinn (SJ Prep) had 5 catches for 86 yards for N-P
games.recap: After N-P mishandled the opening kickoff, Chris Banks (Northeast) recovered, Sammy Tranks (King) soon nailed a 31-yard field goal and Public never looked back.

games.notes: Bill Edger (SJP) caught 6 passes for 128 yards
highlights: Public scored first and last but got pummeled in between
Bill Edger set receiving yardage record with 128 yards
Mike Varanavage turned 21 carries into 103 yards, 2 TDs
Rockeed McCarter (Roman) blocked punt, scored 1-yard return TD
Daniel Chavis (West) and Colin Wixted scored short-run TDs
Maurice Dantzler (Franklin) and Lamone Fox (Northeast) scored Pub TDs

mvps.name: Bill Edger
mvps.stats: 6 catches for 128 yards, 1 TD (new record), 57 yards on punt returns

games.recap: The Pub scored first and last, and got pummeled in between. Bill Edger (SJ Prep), the son of Southern coach Bill Edger Sr., set a game record for receiving yardage with 128 (on six catches).

games.notes: Kendell Coleman (King) received 4 passes for 135 yards and recorded a 64-yard punt return TD
highlights: Pub jumped to 12-0 lead
Public needed late TD to force OT
Calvin Spires scored game-winning TD on 4th-down run in OT
Kendell Coleman set game receiving yardage record with 132 yards on 4 catches
Coleman had 64-yard punt return TD and 70-yard screen pass TD
Jerrick Jenkins' 12-yard scramble made it 24-24 with 47.5 seconds
John Kennedy (Central) made 2 interceptions
games.recap: The Pub jumped to a 12-0 lead, needed a late TD to force OT, then won it with a 1-yard, fourth-down run by Calvin Spires (Frankford) after holding N-P in the top half of football's version of an inning on an interception by Darrell 'Butta' Brown (Dobbins).

games.notes: Mike Mattei (Chestnut Hill) passed for 3 TDs; Al Desiderio (Roman) caught 4 passes for 140 yards with 2 TD receptions
highlights: Easiest victory in all-star history
Non-Public used strong wind and defense for field position advantage
Al Desiderio made 4 catches for 140 yards (record 3rd consecutive year)
Mike Mattei passed 7-for-9 for 148 yards, 3 TD record
Mattei had feared thumb injury from baseball game but played
Jamir Livingston ran 10 times for 128 yards, 2 TDs
Public could not prevent a pass for entire game
games.recap: The Non-Public used a strong wind and effective defense to win the field position battle again and again in the early going, and wound up rolling to the easiest victory in all-star history.

games.scoring_summary: Non-Public dominated 40-0 with first six possessions producing TDs. Mercy rule invoked with 7:34 remaining in 3Q.
games.notes: Judge players left at halftime for prom after 33-0 lead established.
highlights: N-P won by crush-job: first 6 possessions produced TDs
Mercy rule took effect with 7:34 left in 3rd quarter
Mark Giubilato tied game record for passing yards (175)
Tom Ryan finished with 4 catches for 97 yards, 1 TD
Curtis Drake added 104 yards, 2 TDs
All four Judge participants left at halftime (had prom)
Stacey Hill (Comm Tech) rushed 12 times for 46 yards
games.recap: N-P won by crush-job proportions for the third time in four years. Its first six possessions produced TDs and the mercy rule took effect with 7:34 left in the third quarter.

games.scoring_summary: Non-Public won 16-15. Will Huff tied FG record with 3 makes (27, 28, 33 yds). Attendance: 4,936.
games.notes: First meeting at Lincoln Financial Field with 4,936 attendance.
highlights: First meeting at Lincoln Financial Field
Will Huff tied record for field goals with 3 (27, 28, 33 yards)
Huff averaged 38.3 yards on 3 punts
Pete Hurley caught 17-yard TD from Jarred Evans (West Catholic)
Curt Wortham (Judge) produced 115 yards on 21 rushes
Ramadan Abdullah (Germantown) snagged 77-yard TD
Public had 112 yards in penalties
games.recap: The teams' first meeting at Lincoln Financial Field, witnessed by 4,936, produced a hero from nearby 12th and Ritner, Neumann-Goretti's Will Huff tied the record for field goals, with three (27, 28 and 33 yards).

games.scoring_summary: Public reversed 20-16 deficit with two INT return TDs in final 8 minutes. Camille Max 51-yd pick-6; Daquan Cooper 95-yd return TD.
games.notes: Public achieved major comeback with defensive scores.
highlights: Public posted two long TDs on interceptions in final 8 minutes
Camille Max (Northeast) 51-yard pick six
Daquan Cooper (Washington) 95-yard pick six
Jaelen Strong-Rankin set receiving record with 237 yards (previous 140)
John Loughery threw both TD passes to Strong-Rankin (52 and 61 yards)
Tyree Rucker (Fels) 10-for-16, 113 yards
Christian Guinan (Chestnut Hill) kicked 29 and 19-yard FGs
games.recap: Though it surrendered 319 passing yards, Public exacted its revenge in the final eight minutes by posting two long TDs on interceptions to reverse a 20-16 deficit and put the brakes on a four-losses-in-five-years skid.

games.scoring_summary: Public won 29-22. Sharif Smith scored two 1-yard TDs. Multiple special rules violations.
games.notes: Public achieved second consecutive win. Shakur NeSmith ejected in Q2 but reinstated at halftime.
highlights: Public posted second consecutive win for first time since 1983-86
Sharif Smith's first TD came after unsuccessful 4th-down do-over
Shakur NeSmith ejected in 2nd quarter but reinstated at halftime
N-P drew within one at 1:06 with Rob Daniels-to-Bruce Mapp TD
Howard Lynn punted leftfooted, kicked rightfooted (kickoffs/PATs)
Michael Brown, Andre Shirley, Khalil Scott had interceptions
games.recap: Sharif Smith (Furness) burrowed for a pair of 1-yard TDs and his QB partner, Marquise Brown (Bok), passed for two more scores as Public posted a second consecutive win for the first time since capturing four in a row from 1983 to 86.

highlights: Public claiming third consecutive win for first time since 1983-86
Vito Goggins (Bok) had 15 tackles
Antoine Whitney turned 2nd lateral on kickoff into 79-yard TD
Renz Compton (Frankford) fumble recovery setup Brunson's first TD
Game played at Northeast High under steady second-half rain
Dennis Boice (Judge) scored only N-P TD on 1-yard run

mvps.name: Antoine Whitney
mvps.stats: 79-yard TD on kickoff lateral, 32 yards on 5 catches

games.recap: While claiming a third consecutive win for the first time since a four-year run from 1983-86, Public received gigantic performances by guys from schools about to close. Bok was repped by Vittorio 'Vito' Goggins -- he numbered eight solos among 15 tackles (five behind the line).

highlights: First time Non-Public allowed players from Catholic schools outside city
Non-Public's 5 TDs all scored by outside-city players
Chris Gary scored 55-yard pick six on final play of first half
Jylil Reeder (Fels) made 3 catches for 144 yards including 80-yard TD
Jordan Alexander (King) led Public with 9 tackles
games.recap: For the first time, game brass permitted the Non-Public team to use players from Catholic schools outside the city limits and the newbies certainly made a difference, accounting for all five of their team's TDs.

highlights: ALL-time finish: Paul Dooley hit John Hall (Wood) for TD with 8-10ths second left
Dan McDonald (Wood) kicked 35-yard PAT
Four TDs in final 4:57
Kevin Caldwell set passing yardage record (183 previous)
Alex Arcangeli broke rushing yardage record (139 previous)
N-P final drive covered 87 yards
Public had three 15-yard penalties for special violations

mvps.name: Paul Dooley
mvps.stats: Winning TD pass with 8-10ths second remaining

games.recap: This one featured an ALL-time finish. With eight-10ths of a second remaining, after a violation of special game rules concerning alignments/actions had pushed back the ball from the 5 to the 20, Paul Dooley (SCH Academy) bobbed, weaved and bobbed some more before hitting a prone John Hall (Wood) for a TD to tie the score.

games.mvp: James Gillespie (offense), Gianni Cruel (defense), Anthony Russo (sportsmanship)
highlights: Public eclipsed record for points by one team (58)
Non-Public scored enough to also break combined record (92 total)
Shayne Smith passed for record-tying 3 TDs
Public led 38-13 at halftime
DeJason Ellis broke PAT record with 7 conversions
Anthony Russo (Wood) threw for 2 TDs in 261-yard effort (22-for-36)
Combined 857 scrimmage yards, 606 in air, 38 first downs, 177 penalty yards
games.recap: The Pub eclipsed the record for points by one team and the Non-Pub came up with enough offense of its own to make sure the total mark also was subjected to an eraser. Shayne Smith (Del-Val) passed 10-for-19 for 305 yards for a record-tying three TDs.

highlights: Non-Pub owned slim halftime lead (2 points) and big 3rd quarter lead (19 points)
All three TD plays in 3rd quarter covered 34 yards
Mark Webb (Wood) caught TD from Anthony Butler
Shawn Thompson and Ahmad Kent (West Catholic) caught TDs from Hnatkowsky
Danny Bentley (Conwell-Egan) fumble recovery setup last TD
Cyree Boggs (West Catholic) recovered fumble and 2 tackles for losses
games.recap: Non-Pub owned a slim lead (two points) at halftime and a big one (19 points) after three quarters while bouncing back from a 24-point spanking in 2016. No game records were broken/tied, but the teams did combine for 220 yards on 28 penalties.

highlights: Public roared to final 26 points, winning for 5th time in 8 years
298 yards on 32 penalties combined
Mahmud Dioubate tied game record for passing TDs with 3
Talid Crippen scored on 47-yard fumble return after bad punt snap
Da'Vion Kidd-Jackson passed to James Cherry and Dahmir Ruffin
Anthony Tigano (SJ Prep) hit two field goals
games.recap: Public roared to the final 26 points while winning for the fifth time in eight years and reducing its disadvantage in the series to 23-20-1. Somehow, the teams combined for 298 yards on 32 penalties, thus topping 2017's numbers (220-28).

highlights: Tarik Bey (Bartram) scored on QB sneak with 45 seconds remaining
Jordan Price (Frankford) added two-point conversion
Yusuf Terry (Imhotep) caught 28-yard TD from Bey
Jacir Savoy (West Catholic) scampered 30 yards for TD
Jahlil Sanders (Ryan) raced 40 yards for TD on trick play
games.recap: After being stopped at the 2, then the 1, Public moved within a point with 45 seconds remaining as Tarik Bey (Bartram) powered across on a QB sneak. Angling slightly from right to left within the tackles, tailback Jordan Price (Frankford) added the winning conversion. Non-Public offered no late heroics.
